intro: Federal regulators are backing Trump's plan to expedite power connections for energy-intensive AI data centers, though states and grid operators express concerns about losing authority. Noam Shazeer, who co-led Google's Gemini project and founded Character.AI, is joining OpenAI in the latest high-profile talent move between tech giants. Yann LeCun, Meta's AI chief, criticized Elon Musk's xAI as a "failure," reigniting their dispute and raising questions about valuations across major AI companies.

whitelist.title: Federal regulators back Trump's plan to speed power to energy-hungry AI data centers
whitelist.desc: As tech companies and developers welcome faster power supply connections, states and operators worry that the plan would dim their authority.

whitelist.title: A Google veteran who founded character.AI is jumping to OpenAI
whitelist.desc: Noam Shazeer, a co-lead at Gemini and the founder of Character.AI is leaving Google to join OpenAI in the latest AI talent war move.

whitelist.title: Google Gemini co-lead Noam Shazeer leaves for OpenAI
whitelist.desc: Google's vice president of engineering and a co-leader of its Gemini AI models announced Wednesday he is leaving the company to join OpenAI.

whitelist.title: Co-lead of Gemini joins OpenAI.
whitelist.desc: Noam Shazeer spent twenty years at Google before returning in 2024 after the Big G reportedly paid Character.AI — a company co-founded by Shazeer in 2021 — $2.7 billion to bring him and a team of researchers back home. [Media: https://twitter.com/NoamShazeer/status/2067400851438932297?s=20]

whitelist.title: Godfather of AI blasts Musk's xAI as 'failure,' says labs are risking a 'big bubble explosion'
whitelist.desc: Yann LeCun's comments renew a long-running spat with Musk and cast doubt over valuations of some of the world's biggest AI companies.

whitelist.title: JPMorgan cuts off Anthropic access for Hong Kong staff
whitelist.desc: JPMorgan has removed Anthropic’s Claude from its approved AI tools for Hong Kong staff over licensing terms, the FT reports.
